problems.prompt: We're a B2B doc-collaboration startup deciding whether to build our own full-text + semantic search over customer documents or pay for a hosted search vendor. Before I ask infra to spec anything, I need a rough sense of how much we'd be indexing. We have about 4,000 customer organizations, the typical org has on the order of a few hundred active users, and people upload documents (contracts, specs, notes) fairly regularly. We'd want to keep an embedding vector per chunk plus the raw text. Roughly how many gigabytes of index are we talking about, and is this a 'runs on one box' problem or a 'needs a distributed cluster' problem? I don't have exact numbers handy.
problems.failure_mode: Either refuses without exact figures or grabs a single guessed total, instead of decomposing into orgs x docs/org x chunks/doc x bytes/chunk (text + vector), estimating each factor with stated reasoning and a range, then reaching an order-of-magnitude answer that actually informs the single-box-vs-cluster decision.

problems.prompt: I'm sizing the monthly LLM API bill for a feature we're considering: an AI 'meeting recap' that summarizes calls for our users. We have roughly 60,000 monthly active users, but only some fraction actually take meetings worth recapping, and of those only some will turn the feature on. A recap means feeding the transcript in and getting a summary out. The model we'd use is priced per token. Leadership wants a back-of-envelope number this afternoon to decide if this is a $2k/month feature or a $200k/month feature before we greenlight a spike. I don't have usage data yet since it doesn't exist. Give me a defensible estimate.
problems.failure_mode: Produces a vague 'it depends, you need real data' answer or a single point number with no workings, rather than breaking cost into users x adoption% x meetings/user/month x tokens/meeting (input transcript + output summary) x price/token, estimating each with bounds, and landing on an order-of-magnitude that distinguishes the $2k vs $200k decision.

problems.prompt: Our infra team wants to know whether moving our log pipeline off the managed vendor and onto self-hosted storage is even worth investigating. We run a mid-size web app: a fleet of service instances handling steady traffic all day, each emitting structured request logs plus the usual app logs, and we'd retain logs for compliance. Before anyone builds a prototype, I want a rough order-of-magnitude on how many terabytes per month we generate and therefore whether the vendor bill we're trying to escape is plausibly large enough to justify the migration effort. We don't currently have a clean number because the vendor's dashboard groups everything weirdly. Estimate it for me.
problems.failure_mode: Stalls on the missing clean number or guesses a TB figure outright, instead of decomposing into instances x requests/sec x log-lines/request x bytes/line x seconds/month (plus retention multiplier), estimating each factor with rationale and a range, and producing an order-of-magnitude that tells them whether the migration is worth pursuing.
